issue_summary: Belt is slipping and making a loud squeaking noise.
troubleshooting_steps: Step 1: Initiate Level 2 LOTO on Main Disconnect Panel 4. Step 2: Verify zero energy state using a Fluke T6 multimeter. Step 3: Inspect primary drive tensioner pulley (Part #TP-892); found worn ABEC-7 sleeve bearings. Step 4: Replace tensioner pulley and realign anti-static belt to 0.5 deg tolerance. Step 5: Torque mounting bolts to 45 Nm. Step 6: Remove LOTO and perform test run monitoring amp draw via VFD.

issue_summary: Spindle speed is inconsistent and vibrating.
troubleshooting_steps: Step 1: Isolate 480V 3-phase power to the spindle drive subsystem and establish LOTO. Step 2: Connect SKF Microlog vibration analyzer to spindle housing; detected harmonic imbalance at 4000 RPM (1X vibration). Step 3: Remove spindle cover and inspect angular contact ceramic bearings (SKF series 71924). Step 4: Replaced scored front bearings and re-greased with Kluber NBU 15. Step 5: Perform laser alignment and run 2-hour spindle warm-up cycle.

issue_summary: Arm is jittery during the pick-and-place movement.
troubleshooting_steps: Step 1: Secure robotic cell perimeter and physically lock teach pendant into T1 (manual) mode. Step 2: Query servo logs for Joint 4 via the KUKA KRC4 controller; found active encoder drift and following error faults. Step 3: Recalibrate absolute encoder positions using the electronic mastering diode (EMD). Step 4: Inspect base cast iron mounting bolts and retorque to 120 Nm using calibrated torque wrench. Step 5: Run pick-and-place diagnostic routine at 20% TCP speed.

issue_summary: Output pressure is dropping below 80 PSI.
troubleshooting_steps: Step 1: Shut down rotary screw compressor and bleed air from the receiver tank to 0 PSI. Step 2: Inspect air intake filter housing for particulate blockage. Step 3: Perform ultrasonic leak detection on primary discharge valve block; identified continuous high-frequency leak. Step 4: Isolate valve block and replace Viton O-ring seals (Kit #OR-550). Step 5: Restart unit and verify it builds to 120 PSI operational setpoint within 45 seconds.

issue_summary: Making a loud rattling noise from the blower.
troubleshooting_steps: Step 1: Disconnect 480V 3-phase power from the variable frequency drive (VFD) and apply LOTO tags. Step 2: Open blower access panel and inspect squirrel cage for balancing weights. Step 3: Found loose sheet metal debris from ductwork and a missing balancing clip. Step 4: Remove debris and rebalance the centrifugal wheel using a dynamic balancer. Step 5: Lubricate pillow block bearings with high-temp lithium-based grease.

issue_summary: Plastic parts showing burn marks and discoloration.
troubleshooting_steps: Step 1: Halt 60-second production cycle and purge remaining polycarbonate resin from the heated barrel. Step 2: Inspect heater bands on Zone 3 using a FLIR thermal imaging camera; found Band 3 running 35C above PLC setpoint. Step 3: Check solid-state relay (SSR) for Zone 3 in electrical cabinet; SSR found fused in the closed circuit position. Step 4: De-energize cabinet (LOTO) and replace 40A SSR (Part #SSR-40DA). Step 5: Verify PID control loops reach steady-state 220C.

issue_summary: Intermittent power failure causing line stoppage.
troubleshooting_steps: Step 1: Consult Allen-Bradley electrical schematic #E-104 for Panel 1. Step 2: Measure input voltage at main breaker (expected 240VAC nominal). Step 3: Measure DC output of 24VDC power supply (MeanWell NDR-240 series); found unstable output rippling between 18V-24V under load. Step 4: Depower panel swap faulty SMPS power supply unit. Step 5: Repower and monitor diagnostic LEDs on ControlLogix PLC rack for solid green OK status.

Step 1: Power down Lincoln Electric welding power source and isolate Argon/CO2 gas supply. Step 2: Extract and inspect welding torch liner for copper-coated steel shavings and carbon buildup. Step 3: Remove and replace the 0.045" teflon liner and copper contact tip. Step 4: Adjust serrated drive roll tension on the wire feeder to manufacturer specs (Dial 3). Step 5: Perform destructive test weld on scrap ASTM A36 steel to verify consistent arc stability and penetration.
